title: linum tenuifolium subsp. ortegae
intro: Linum tenuifolium subsp. ortegae, also known as the narrowleaf flax or prairie flax, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum tenuifolium subsp. ortegae include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Linum tenuifolium subsp. ortegae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
